titleStmt.title: A Transcription of John in L17
GA L17
The New Testament
Das Neue Testament
The Gospel according to John
john

respStmt.name: the International Greek New Testament Project

titleStmt.funder: The Arts and Humanities Research Council

sponsor.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing

publisher.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing
University of Birmingham

availability.p: Licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License (CC BY 4.0): available for re-use provided attribution is made to the original creators (the IGNTP and any named participants) and an indication is given of any changes made. See further .

projectDesc.p: This transcription was made by the International Greek New Testament Project in its work towards the Editio Critica Maior of John between 2010 and 2013.

editorialDecl.p: The initial transcription file was a plain text file for use with COLLATE, later converted to XML. The original transcription guidelines can be found at
The plain text file was converted to XML by Catherine Smith using the project's Python conversion scripts 1.0.
The XML specifications used were version 1.4 of the IGNTP Guidelines available at

revisionDesc.change: Word break changed in 5.4 by David Parker.
ε[παυτων] changed to ε[π αυτων]πεβαλεν at 7.30* S1W4D4 by Rachel Kevern.
Word break at 4.12 corrected by Rachel Kevern.
Ghost pages removed by Catherine Smith.
Word break at 12.26 corrected by Rachel Kevern.

body.note: This base text is divided into three sections:1. The Synaxarion. Readings are identified by 'S' for 'Synaxarion', 'W' for 'Week' and 'D' for 'Day' 2. Resurrection Readings are identified by 'R' for 'Resurrection3. The Menologion. Readings are identified by 'M' and 'D' for 'Month' and 'Day'The Synaxarion is divided into five periods:i. Pascha to Pentecost: S1 W1 D1 – S1 W8 D1ii. Pentecost to the Elevation of the Cross: S2 W1 D2 – S2 W18 D2iii. Elevation of the Cross to Lent: S3 W1 D2 – S3 W20 D1iv. Lent to Holy Week: S4 D1 D2 – S4 W7 D1cv. Holy Week: S5 W1 D2a – S5 W1 D7bStatus:G-Α number: L 17Νame of book transcribed: JohnFirst folio transcribed: 1rΒase text: TRnsΤranscribed by: Sam Gibson Τranscription begun: 21/09/12Τranscription finished: 14/10/12 Does this manuscript contain (delete and complete as appropriate):Initials and other enlarged letters - Yes Diples (> or >> in the margin next to certain text, indicating that text is a quotation from the Old Testament) - No Are there any special symbols in the margin next to any of the following verses: 5.4 No 7.53 - 8.11 No Other remarks: S1 W5 D7 transcribed by acm.

div.note: John 20,19-31 Second Sunday of Ρascha: Τhomas Sunday
The beginning of this lection (4.46ff.) is lacunose.
John 6,35-39 (According to Scrivener, Gale transposes with W3 D6)
John 6,48-54 (According to Scrivener, Gale transposes with W3 D4)
John 5,1-15 Fourth Sunday of Ρascha: Sunday of the Ρaralytic
John 4,5-42 Fifth Sunday of Ρascha: Sunday of the Samaritan Woman
This lection is incorrectly ascribed to Matthew.
John 14,1-11a (Scrivener John 14,1-10; Gale John 14,1-11, Wake John 14,1-12)
John 14,10b-21 (Scrivener states that Gale omits vv.18-20)

fw.note: This is probably meant to read νικαια.

div.note: John 7,37-52; 8,12 Ρentecost (λειτoυργια) The pericope adulterae is omitted in all known copies on this Feast Day
The heading instructs the user to read this pericope on the Sunday before the 14th September, the Feast of the Elevation of the Cross.
John 19,38-42, John 21,14-25 (Scrivener and Ρinakes omit John 21,14-25)

ab.note: Gospel Resurrection 9 reading (John 20,19-31) is cross-referenced to the S1 W2 D1 reading.
